LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JOSIAH LARKIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SUBSTANTIALLY OPPOSED. IMPROPER SERVICE IS WAIVED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACTS AS TO THE LEASE BEING SUPERIOR TO PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST BASED ON OPEN OCCUPANCY BY TENANTS. IN ANY EVENT, THE PROPERTY IS GOVERNED BY THE RENT ACT EVICTION CONTROL PROVISIONS. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING A BASIS FOR A CONTINUANCE UNDER CCP 437C(H). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, COURT REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Josiah Larkin, 415-240-6297, Defendant and moving party in Pro Per
Mariel A. Gerlt, Esq., Alvarado Smith, 415-852-6800, for plaintiff and opposing party

ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT JOSIAH LARKIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted because Plaintiff substantially opposed. Improper service is waived. Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material facts as to the lease being superior to Plaintiff's interest based on open occupancy by tenants. In any event, the property is governed by the Rent Act Eviction Control Provisions. Finally, Plaintiff has not met its burden of showing a basis for a continuance under CCP 437C(h). Prevailing party to submit a form of order in compliance with CCP 437C(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Court Reporter: Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)
